steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures on each side of the blank
steps.content: Before the blank: "Okinaka doesn't make such decisions" is an independent clause. After the blank: "all historical designations must be approved by a group of nine other experts" is also an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the independent clause boundary rule
steps.content:
Rule: two independent clauses must be separated by a semicolon, a period, or a comma + coordinating conjunction (FANBOYS). Left: "Okinaka doesn't make such decisions single-handedly." Right: "all historical designations must be approved by a group of nine other experts."

A: "...single-handedly and all historical designations..." — "and" without a preceding comma attempts to join two independent clauses; a comma before "and" is required. Incorrect.

B: "...single-handedly, all historical designations..." — a comma alone joining two independent clauses is a comma splice. Incorrect.

C: "...single-handedly all historical designations..." — no punctuation at all creates a run-on sentence. Incorrect.

D: "...single-handedly; all historical designations..." — the semicolon correctly joins the two closely related independent clauses. Correct.
